assertions: Produces a design brief or design document, not a finished SKILL.md implementation
Names trigger phrasings and at least one nearby request that should NOT trigger the skill
Declares the weakest model or agent the skill must work for (a floor), not just the best case
Treats the repo's commitlint configuration as a source of truth to point to, rather than copying its scope list into the skill
Lists concrete checks or evals to build before or alongside the skill
Surfaces unknowns as explicit open questions instead of silently assuming answers
prompt: i keep hand-writing conventional commit messages for this repo and when teammates use claude half the scopes come out wrong. we already have commitlint set up (see commitlint config in the repo root). I want a skill the whole team can drop into claude code that gets commit messages right on the first try. before we build anything, help me design it properly — I don't want a SKILL.md yet, I want the design worked out first.
